QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_27

question.text: A trader sells 10 lots of NIFTY futures at 25,200 (lot size 25). NIFTY closes at 25,350. What is the day's MTM?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short position MTM = (Sell price - Settlement price) × Lot size × Lots = (25,200 - 25,350) × 25 × 10 = -150 × 250 = -Rs 37,500 (loss). The trader is short, so a price rise causes a loss. **The Trap:** Profit would apply if the price had fallen. Rs 3,750 uses 1 lot. Rs 375,000 multiplies by an extra factor of 10. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch3 covers NIFTY futures P&L. The 25 lot size is standard for NIFTY futures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 150-point move is about 0.6% of NIFTY — but on a margin of ~Rs 1.5-2 lakh per lot, this Rs 37,500 loss represents about 2-2.5% of margin per lot. Leverage amplifies losses.
